author: codex-dup-audit

patch.value: Deduplicate front-matter key-order contract literals in tests

Duplicate audit 2026-05-22: jscpd found tests/unit/shared-constants-errors.spec.ts copies the full FRONT_MATTER_KEY_ORDER array from src/core/shared/constants.ts. Representative clone: constants.ts:46-115 vs shared-constants-errors.spec.ts:53-122. The literal copy catches accidental key-order changes, but it is large and easy to update mechanically without noticing intended contract changes. Existing pm-why9 covers default status/workflow model duplication, not this front-matter contract literal. Fix by replacing the long copied array with a smaller contract fixture strategy: either a named snapshot/golden file with clear update intent, or assertions over stable sentinel ordering and required membership that preserve contract value without a full in-test copy.

shared-constants tests no longer inline-copy the entire FRONT_MATTER_KEY_ORDER array; the replacement still catches accidental front-matter ordering or missing required keys; serialization/release-readiness expectations remain deterministic; focused shared-constants tests and pnpm build pass.

message: Create duplicate-audit finding for front-matter key-order test literals

value.author: codex-dup-audit
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: before creating, searched existing items for FRONT_MATTER_KEY_ORDER/shared constants/key-order test duplication. Existing pm-why9 covers runtime-schema vs constants defaults, not front-matter key-order contract literals. jscpd and manual inspection confirmed constants.ts:46-115 duplicates shared-constants-errors.spec.ts:53-122.

patch.value: Closed by replacing the copied front-matter key-order literal with focused contract assertions.
Shared-constants tests preserve front-matter ordering guarantees without duplicating the full production key-order array.
Item closed after the duplicate literal was removed and focused shared-constants/build verification passed.
